basics.name: Rohit Prakash
basics.label: Embedded Systems Engineer | M.Sc. ICT @ FAU Erlangen
basics.summary: Engineer passionate about embedded systems, wireless technologies, and clean firmware design. Experienced across product validation, peripheral interface testing, and real-world systems optimization.

work.name: Exasol AG
work.summary: Power BI automation, Salesforce support, and internal debugging for IT operations.
highlights: Built internal Power BI dashboards for data-driven decisions
Contributed to web debugging and internal tools

work.name: Microchip Technology
work.summary: Developed and validated firmware applications for wireless MCUs and regulatory certifications.
highlights: Implemented WPA2/WPA3 Enterprise security protocols using TLS/TTLS and FreeRADIUS
Designed UART flow control system supporting up to 25 Mbps
Built QSPI/SQI firmware for high-speed flash memory operations
Assisted in Class-B functional safety and CE/FCC certification processes
Created multiprotocol test apps for UART, I2C, SPI, ADC, PWM, Ethernet, Wi-Fi

work.name: Infosys Ltd.
work.summary: Developed backend APIs using Spring Boot and MuleSoft for HSBC client.
highlights: Worked on API integration and backend scripting
Collaborated on banking application documentation via Confluence
Contributed to agile team delivery and defect resolution

projects.name: Wi-Fi Enterprise Security Mode
projects.summary: Developed WPA2/WPA3 enterprise protocols with FreeRADIUS integration.

projects.name: UART Flow Control
projects.summary: Validated high-speed UART flow control with RTS/CTS up to 25 Mbps.

projects.name: QSPI/SQI Memory Integration
projects.summary: Built and validated high-speed QSPI/SQI drivers on SST26 flash.

projects.name: Product Regulatory Certification
projects.summary: Developed multiprotocol test apps (Wi-Fi, ADC, SPI, I2C, etc.) for CE/FCC.

projects.name: Class-B Functional Safety
projects.summary: Validated IEC 60730 Class-B safety mechanisms for embedded MCUs.
